data.title: "46% Upside Potential": Here's Why This Top Analyst Is Bullish on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Citi analyst Atif Malik is bullish on Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report on February 25, pointing to strong product momentum and improving demand visibility that stretches well into 2027. He reiterated a Buy rating on NVDA stock and a $270 price target, which implies 46% upside potential.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The five-star analyst expects Nvidia to deliver another standout quarter, projecting January‑quarter revenue of $67 billion, comfortably above the Street's consensus of $65.6 billion. He also anticipates April‑quarter sales guidance of $73 billion, topping analyst estimates of $71.6 billion.

Analyst Sees Stronger Growth Ahead

Malik's optimism is driven by Nvidia's continued ramp of its B300 and Rubin platforms, which he believes will fuel a 34% acceleration in the second half of 2026 sales, compared with 27% growth in the first half.

Malik also forecasts Fiscal 2027 gross margins of around 75%. Moreover, he expects operating expense growth to remain in the high‑30% range, similar to Fiscal 2026.

He noted that most investors are already looking beyond the quarter toward Nvidia's annual GTC conference in mid-March, where the company is expected to outline its next-generation inference roadmap, including the use of Groq's low-latency SRAM IP.

Further, the analyst believes hyperscaler spending, while a concern for some investors, will ultimately deliver long-term returns as AI-driven infrastructure demand accelerates cloud revenue growth.

NVDA's Q4 Earnings and Revenue Expected to Jump Y/Y

Currently, analysts expect NVDA to post revenue of $65.58 billion in Q4, up from $39.33 billion in the year-ago quarter. Further, the company is expected to report earnings of $1.52 per share, compared with earnings of $0.89 in the prior-year quarter.

The market will be looking for updates on the rollout of Nvidia's latest platforms and any early signals about its 2026 product roadmap. Also, the company's guidance will play a major role in shaping sentiments around the broader AI and semiconductor sectors.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 379 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Intel Looks for "One in Two" PCs to be AI-Driven; Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips Regardless
data.description:
The notion of an artificial intelligence (AI) PC has caught some imaginations, but has not made a lot of headway in the market. But chip stock Intel (INTC) looks for the AI PC phenomenon to take off in a very big way, eventually. Intel looks for one out of every two PCs to be AI PCs, and it looks for Panther Lake to lead the way therein. Investors were skeptical. They sent Intel shares down fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, Intel is convinced that its Panther Lake lineup is perfect for the AI PC market. So much so, in fact, that with Panther Lake chips in these devices, their adoption rate would soar, ultimately reaching right around half of the overall PC market. Most AI PCs have suffered from a lack of overall computing power, but the Panther Lake lineup looks to deliver the necessary power, and thus improve adoption rates.

Intel Japan CEO Makoto Ohno noted, "It is also predicted that by 2026, this will exceed half, with one in two computers being an AI PC. However, given the current situation, reasons for choosing an AI PC include its high performance and the long battery life provided by the use of an NPU. In other words, it is important to reflect on the fact that people are not currently purchasing an AI PC in order to use its AI-related functions."

Xe Next

That is not all that is up Intel's collective sleeve, though. Intel is currently working on the Xe Next lineup, which is the next generation of graphics processing unit (GPU) property. It has no official name as yet, and there is not much information surrounding what it can do. But considering that it is set to be geared toward data centers, reports note, it suggests that Xe Next will be a significant power in the market.

But Xe Next will come after many of Intel's current products, like the Jaguar Shores lineup. Reports suggest that Jaguar Shores will be ready in the middle of the year, with a potential release either late this year, or early in 2027. And Intel is also working on further Arc releases, the Celestial and the Druid. Getting these into the field should augment Intel's performance at a time when it sorely needs new market share.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 70.83% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 3.57% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Gold's Historic Surge and the Volatility Test Ahead
data.description:
Gold's (XAUUSD) recent spot performance has been nothing short of extraordinary. In 2025, it delivered a record-breaking run—posting 53 new all-time highs and pushing global demand beyond 5,000 tonnes for the first time. That surge translated into a historic demand value of $555 billion, up 45% year-over-year, marking the first time annual gold demand has exceeded both 5,000 tonnes and $500 billion.

data.title: "The Solar Opportunity is Underestimated": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slides as Buffalo Workforce Ramping Up
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) may not be an electric vehicle giant much longer. It has made a lot of strides to diversify of late, and one of the big new things Tesla is pushing into is solar panels. In fact, reports suggest that Tesla's Buffalo workforce is getting some serious augmentation in the face of a potentially growing opportunity. Investors proved skeptical, though, judging by the nearly 2.5% drop in share prices in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla wants to roll out 100 gigawatts' worth of solar panels annually, reports note, a goal that is by any stretch ambitious. Tesla first got into solar when it acquired Solar City, but was only set to produce a bare hundredth of that number, just one gigawatt annually. But Tesla has been working, and recently revealed it has a new solar panel with not only more attractive looks, but also better performance in "partially shaded areas."

Tesla's own Elon Musk noted, "The solar opportunity is underestimated. We're gonna work towards getting 100 gigawatts a year of solar cell production, integrating across the entire supply chain, from raw materials all the way to finished solar panels." It would not be the first time a publicly-traded company went into solar panels, but few of them have done that well.

Kicking Tesla While It's Down

Many hope that Musk is right here, because the cars certainly have seen better days as far as sales go. A new story is going viral through Chinese state media about a Tesla Model Y that, somehow, managed to lose all power on the highway despite the fact it had significant range left to its battery, according to the cabin display.

The fact that Chinese media is state-controlled suggests that this story was deliberately amplified, reports note. This suggests a mood shift about Tesla in the mind of the Chinese government. Given how many of Tesla's competitors in the electric vehicle space are Chinese in nature, it is not outside the realm of possibility that the Chinese government may be taking a minor incident and using it as a goad to whip Tesla out of the market.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.88% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 1.9% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Big Investors, Including Warren Buffett, Trim 'Magnificent Seven' Stakes in Q4
data.description:
Major investors are starting to pull back from some of the market's biggest technology winners. Recent 13-F filings show several hedge funds and large institutions reduced holdings in "Magnificent Seven" companies — including Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Alphabet (GOOGL) — during the fourth quarter of 2025, mainly after a sharp AI-driven rally pushed valuations higher and raised questions about how quickly heavy AI spending will turn into profits.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tiger Global Trims Key AI Positions

Tiger Global, a large U.S.-based hedge fund, reduced its Microsoft holding to 5.47 million shares from 6.5 million shares. Even after the cut, the stake is still worth about $2.6 billion and remains one of its largest positions.

It also:

  • cut its Amazon stake by roughly 9% to about 10 million shares (around $2.3 billion)
  • trimmed its Nvidia position, now valued near $2.1 billion

Adage Capital Trims Big Tech, Increases Oracle Bet

Meanwhile, hedge fund Adage Capital made smaller reductions of roughly 1%–3% in its holdings of Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Nvidia.

However, the fund increased investment elsewhere in the AI ecosystem. It boosted its stake in Oracle (ORCL) by about 19% to nearly 1.87 million shares, valued at roughly $365 million.

Warren Buffett Cuts Apple and Amazon Stakes

Several large investors made similar adjustments.  Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) continued to trim its stakes in Apple and sold a large portion of its Amazon stake. Meanwhile, the fund opened a new position in The New York Times Co.'s (NYT) stock in the fourth quarter of 2025 just before the shares rallied to an all-time high.

Separately, SoftBank disclosed it had sold its Nvidia stake earlier to raise cash for further investment in OpenAI. 

Which Magnificent 7 Stock Is the Better Buy? 

Now, let's see how Wall Street analysts are weighing in on the above-mentioned names. Analysts think that Microsoft stock has the most room to run. In fact, Microsoft's average price target of $593.38 per share implies 49.5% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.description:

ai-workflows">In terms of guidance, Palo Alto Networks said that it expects EPS of $0.78 to $0.80 on revenue of $2.94 billion to $2.95 billion. That outlook fell short of the $0.92 in earnings per share and sales of $2.60 billion that analysts had penciled in for the company.

data.title: Why AMD Stock Slipped Today and Why One Investor Says 'Buy the Dip'
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares slipped 3% in after-hours trading Wednesday after Nvidia and Meta Platforms unveiled an expanded, multiyear AI infrastructure partnership. The deal, which leans heavily on Nvidia's GPUs, CPUs, and networking technology to power Meta's next-gen data centers, could narrow AMD's chances of carving out a larger foothold within Meta's AI infrastructure buildout.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, while investors fixate on who wins the latest mega-deal, AMD's leadership argues the bigger story is the pace of expansion across the AI market. CEO Dr. Lisa Su projects a 35% CAGR over the next three to five years, fueled by what she calls "accelerating AI momentum," suggesting the opportunity could be large enough to drive meaningful gains for AMD.

That growth thesis is precisely why some investors are turning more constructive. While AMD has long participated in the AI narrative, investor Louis Gerard contends the company is entering a more advantageous phase, pointing to its "successful pivot toward a system-scale model."

"I believe that the company is at an inflection point as AMD shifted from selling individual chips to now delivering integrated rack-scale systems," explains the 5-star investor.

Gerard notes that the company's data center revenues have been growing, and now make up the majority of AMD's sales. This momentum seems likely to continue going forward.

The investor is particularly enthusiastic about AMD's upcoming MI450 series, expected for the second half of the year. He calls this a "pivotal moment for the industry," as its increasing memory and bandwidth-first approach is geared toward addressing some of the key hurdles in LLM training and inference.

"That could position AMD to capture a significant share of the next-gen AI superclusters," adds Gerard.

The sliding share price has made AMD a cheaper buy, though with a P/E in the high forties, it's not exactly in bargain territory. Still, it's also at the cheapest levels since the AI boom started.

"With the growth prospects in consideration, I believe that the company is undervalued, especially given the bullishness of the data center segment that could see its total addressable market reach $1 trillion by 2030," says Gerard, who is upgrading AMD to a Strong Buy. (To watch Louis Gerard's track record, click here)

There's little objection on Wall Street to this viewpoint. AMD stock commands 24 Buy ratings against 8 Holds, earning AMD a Strong Buy consensus. And the conviction isn't subtle – the $286.80 average price target implies upside of ~41% from current levels. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip Slightly after a Muted Trading Session
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down marginally on Tuesday evening after a quiet day on Wall Street. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.15%, 0.09%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 6:20 p.m. EST on February 17.
